overview: Atlas Workflow Platform is a fictitious internal system designed to organize operational requests, track workflow status, and generate structured documentation for stakeholders. This demo shows how technical information can be converted into a professional handoff document.

architecture.description: Simple web interface for users to submit and review workflow items.
Backend service that validates requests and coordinates workflow actions.
Structured storage for users, workflow items, statuses, and audit logs.
Automated PDF output for summaries, handoff notes, and internal documentation.

modules.purpose: Collect workflow requests from internal users.
Track each request through review, approval, and completion.
Generate structured PDF documentation from stored workflow data.
Record important changes for traceability and review.

workflow.title: Submit request
workflow.description: A user creates a new workflow request with required details.

workflow.title: Review request
workflow.description: A reviewer validates the request and assigns ownership.

workflow.title: Update status
workflow.description: The assigned owner updates progress and adds notes.

workflow.title: Generate documentation
workflow.description: The system exports a clean PDF summary for stakeholders.

technical_notes.note: Environment-specific values should be stored outside the source code.
Incoming records should be validated before being stored or exported.
Report sections should be generated from structured input to keep formatting consistent.

appendix_notes: All system details are fictitious and used only as a documentation automation case study.
The same structure can be adapted for APIs, internal tools, onboarding manuals, or project handoff documents.
The goal is to demonstrate repeatable technical documentation without manual formatting.
